mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of introducing default interface methods in Java?

options.text: To provide default behavior for methods
To support lambda expressions
To override methods
To allow interfaces to have state

mcqs.explanation: Default interface methods were introduced to provide a way to add new functionality to existing interfaces without breaking backward compatibility.

mcqs.question: In which version of Java were default interface methods introduced?

options.text: Java 7
Java 8
Java 9
Java 10

mcqs.explanation: Default interface methods were introduced in Java 8 as a significant feature in interface design.

mcqs.question: Can default interface methods be overridden by classes that implement the interface?

options.text: Yes
No
Sometimes
Never

mcqs.explanation: Default interface methods can be overridden by classes that implement the interface to provide a custom implementation.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between default interface methods and abstract methods in interfaces?

options.text: Default methods have an implementation, while abstract methods do not
Default methods are used for lambda expressions, while abstract methods are not
Default methods are used for overriding, while abstract methods are not
Default methods are used for state, while abstract methods are not

mcqs.explanation: Default interface methods have an implementation, while abstract methods do not have an implementation and must be implemented by any class that implements the interface.

mcqs.question: How do you declare a default method within an interface?

options.text: Using the `default` keyword followed by the return type and method name
Using the `abstract` keyword followed by the return type and method name
Using the `static` keyword followed by the return type and method name
Using the `private` keyword followed by the return type and method name

mcqs.explanation: To declare a default method within an interface, you use the `default` keyword followed by the return type and method name.

mcqs.question: What happens when a class implements multiple interfaces with the same default method?

options.text: The class must provide its own implementation of the method
The class can use the default implementation from any of the interfaces
The class can use the default implementation from the first interface it implements
The class cannot implement multiple interfaces with the same default method

mcqs.explanation: When a class implements multiple interfaces with the same default method, it must provide its own implementation of the method to resolve the conflict.

mcqs.question: Can default interface methods be used to support lambda expressions?

options.text: Yes
No
Sometimes
Never

mcqs.explanation: Default interface methods can be used to support lambda expressions by providing a target for lambda expressions.
